title: Prevent initial-load crash from unsafe history.state usage and improve ErrorBoundary diagnostics

requirements.summary: Eliminate the immediate-on-open runtime crash so the landing page reliably renders on first load and hard refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app in a fresh tab (no prior navigation) renders the landing page instead of the ErrorBoundary fallback.
Hard-refresh (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R) does not crash on initial render.
The fix specifically prevents runtime exceptions during initial navigation/history state initialization.

file_operations.description: Make initial mount initialization resilient: guard all reads/writes of window.history.state so no exceptions occur during first render, and ensure isInitialized is always set even when history.state is missing or invalid (prevents the landing page from being blocked by a crash).

Add a safe fail-open behavior for first paint: if navigation-state initialization is delayed/unavailable for any reason, ensure the landing content can still render without throwing (keep existing UX otherwise).

requirements.summary: Harden useExperienceNavigationState history synchronization: never throw on null/non-object history.state and preserve existing state fields when setting mindvault_experience.
acceptanceCriteria: When window.history.state is null/undefined, the hook initializes without throwing and writes a valid state via replaceState.
When window.history.state is an object, the hook keeps existing keys and only adds/updates the mindvault_experience key.
Open/close cycles of the Experience view still work (openExperience sets state true; closeExperience returns to landing) without crashing.
Back/forward navigation updates isExperienceOpen correctly without throwing.

file_operations.description: Implement safe history state merging: treat non-object history.state (null, undefined, primitives) as an empty object; preserve any existing history.state keys when calling replaceState/pushState; and harden popstate handling so event.state null/non-object cannot cause crashes while still updating isExperienceOpen correctly.

requirements.summary: Improve ErrorBoundary fallback diagnostics by allowing users to optionally reveal technical error details without changing the existing user-facing message meaning.
acceptanceCriteria: The ErrorBoundary fallback still shows the same headline, body copy, and "Try Again" button text.
The fallback provides an optional way to view technical details (at minimum: error name/message and a short stack excerpt if available).
Technical details are not shown by default; users must expand/reveal them.
Error details shown match the actual thrown error captured by the ErrorBoundary state.

file_operations.description: Extend the fallback UI to include a collapsed-by-default technical details section (e.g., native details/summary) that shows the captured error name/message and a short stack excerpt when available, while keeping the existing headline/body copy and "Try Again" button text unchanged in meaning.
